ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who posted the tweet */,
  "lang" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "replied_to" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet to which this tweet is replying */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the tweet was created */,
  "tweet_text" TEXT /* Text content of the tweet */,
  "possibly_sensitive" INTEGER /* Indicates if the tweet contains sensitive content */,
  "conversation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the conversation thread to which the tweet belongs */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been retweeted */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies to the tweet */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes the tweet has received */,
  "quote_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been quoted */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographic location where the tweet was posted */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet (e.g., Twitter for iPhone, Twitter Web App) */,
  "is_retweet" INTEGER /* Indicates if the tweet is a retweet */,
  "retweet_of_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original tweet if it is a retweet */,
  "hashtags" TEXT /* Hashtags used in the tweet */,
  "mentions" TEXT /* User mentions in the tweet */,
  "media_urls" TEXT /* URLs of media (images, videos) attached to the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES authors ("author_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_conversation_id FOREIGN KEY ("conversation_id") REFERENCES conversations ("conversation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "handle" TEXT /* The author's unique handle or username */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biographical information about the author */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the author has */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* Number of authors the user follows */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL of the author's profile image */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the author is verified */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the author joined the platform */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "conversations" (
  "conversation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each conversation */,
  "root_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the root tweet that started the conversation */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the conversation */,
  "participants_count" INTEGER /* Number of unique participants in the conversation */,
  "last_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the last tweet in the conversation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("conversation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been retweeted */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies to the tweet */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes the tweet has received */,
  "quote_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been quoted */,
  "engagement_score" REAL /* A composite score combining all engagement metrics */,
  "engagement_date" TEXT /* Date when the engagement metrics were last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_engagement_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_sentiment" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the tweet (e.g., -1.0 to 1.0) */,
  "sentiment_label" TEXT /* Sentiment label (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "sentiment_confidence" REAL /* Confidence level of the sentiment analysis */,
  "sentiment_analysis_date" TEXT /* Date when the sentiment analysis was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_sentiment_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last user login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User account status (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the tweet */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the tweet was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet for which the report was generated */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., engagement, sentiment) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the report was created */,
  "report_file" TEXT /* File path to the generated report */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Date and time when the report was last modified */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., generated, in progress) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the permission was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role-permission mapping */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_permissions_permission_id FOREIGN KEY ("permission_id") REFERENCES permissions ("permission_id")
)
